oligos.name: RT primer (WTA)
oligos.role: Reverse transcription primer; primes first-strand cDNA on the mRNA poly(A) tail and adds the WTA tag + T7 promoter to the cDNA 5' end

components.name: WTA tag / PCR target region (M; EcoRI+NotI)
components.role: Common adapter/PCR-target region used for suppression PCR

components.name: T7 promoter
components.role: T7 RNA polymerase promoter (enables IVT for Quartz-Chip)

oligos.notes: HPLC-purified. Identical primer used for the qPCR (non-WTA) RT reaction.

oligos.name: Tagging primer
oligos.role: Second-strand synthesis primer; anneals to the TdT-added poly(A) tail on first-strand cDNA and adds the WTA tag to the other end

components.name: WTA tag / PCR target region (M; EcoRI+NotI)
oligo-dT24
components.role: Anneals to the poly(A) tail added to first-strand cDNA

oligos.notes: HPLC-purified. Lacks the T7 promoter; only the tag + poly-dT.

oligos.name: Suppression PCR primer (B primer)
oligos.role: Single PCR primer for WTA enrichment; suppression PCR that amplifies full-length cDNA while short byproducts self-anneal into a pan-like structure
oligos.notes: 5' NH2(C6) aminated (blocks ligation to TruSeq adapter). 'B primer' (one extra G vs the 'A primer' TATAGAATTCGCGGCCGCTCGCGAT). HPLC-purified; Sigma-Aldrich or Takara-Clontech recommended. 5' amination not required if Nextera/Nextera XT is used for library prep.

oligos.name: TruSeq universal adapter (TRSU)
oligos.role: P5-side TruSeq adapter oligo (universal strand of the Y-adapter)
oligos.notes: HPLC-purified. 3'-terminal phosphorothioate bond (...CCGATC*T). Self-produced TruSeq adapter.

oligos.name: TruSeq indexed adapter (TRSI)
oligos.role: P7-side TruSeq adapter oligo carrying the i7 sample index (index strand of the Y-adapter)

components.name: TruSeq Read 2 adapter
components.role: Read 2 / index sequencing primer binding site

oligos.notes: HPLC-purified. 5'-phosphate; 3'-terminal phosphorothioate (...GCTT*G). Index sequences: TRSI-1 ATCACG, TRSI-2 CGATGT, TRSI-3 TTAGGC, TRSI-4 TGACCA, TRSI-5 ACAGTG, TRSI-6 GCCAAT, TRSI-7 CAGATC, TRSI-8 ACTTGA, TRSI-9 GATCAG, TRSI-10 TAGCTT, TRSI-11 GGCTAC, TRSI-12 CTTGTA.

oligos.name: TruSeq adapter (annealed, double-stranded Y-adapter)
oligos.role: Ligated sequencing adapter formed by annealing TRSU + one TRSI index oligo

components.name: TRSU (universal strand)
components.role: P5 arm of the forked adapter

components.name: TRSI (index strand)
components.role: P7/index arm of the forked adapter

oligos.notes: Annealed by heating equal amounts of 100 uM TRSU + TRSI to 95C then ramping -0.5C/cycle x170; forms a Y-shaped adapter with a ligatable T-overhang.

oligos.name: Library PCR primer TPC1 (P5)
oligos.notes: HPLC-purified. 3'-terminal phosphorothioate (...CCGA*G). Corresponds to Illumina P5.

oligos.name: Library PCR primer TPC2 (P7)
oligos.notes: HPLC-purified. 3'-terminal phosphorothioate (...TACGA*G). Corresponds to Illumina P7.

oligos.name: Illumina P5 adapter
oligos.role: P5 flow-cell grafting sequence (5' end of final library)
oligos.notes: Contained within TRSU / primed by TPC1.

oligos.name: Illumina P7 adapter
oligos.role: P7 flow-cell grafting sequence (3' end of final library, appears as reverse complement on the top strand)
oligos.notes: Contained within TRSI (as ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG revcomp) / primed by TPC2.

oligos.name: Illumina TruSeq Read 1 sequencing primer
oligos.notes: Standard Illumina TruSeq Read 1 primer (binding site supplied by TRSU; sequence not separately printed in the protocol).

oligos.name: Illumina TruSeq Read 2 sequencing primer
oligos.notes: Standard Illumina TruSeq Read 2 primer (binding site supplied by TRSI; sequence not separately printed in the protocol).

oligos.name: Illumina TruSeq index sequencing primer
oligos.notes: Standard Illumina TruSeq index-read primer (binding site supplied by TRSI; sequence not separately printed in the protocol).

final_library.source_label: Quartz-Seq WTA cDNA + LIMprep TruSeq paired-end library

evidence.locator: Quartz-Seq WTA cDNA + LIMprep TruSeq paired-end library

library_generation.title: Reverse transcription (first-strand cDNA)
library_generation.summary: SuperScript III extends the oligo-dT RT primer on the mRNA poly(A) tail, tagging the cDNA 5' end with the WTA tag + T7 promoter.
library_generation.note: Single cell lysed in 0.5% NP-40; RT primer denatured/annealed (70C/35C) then extended at 35C->45C. The RT primer's oligo-dT24 anneals to the mRNA poly(A) tail.

library_generation.title: Primer digestion (Exonuclease I)
library_generation.summary: Exo I digests residual single-stranded RT primer to prevent byproduct synthesis.
library_generation.note: 37C 30 min then heat-inactivated. Removes most (not all) unextended RT primer; the cDNA is protected as a duplex.

library_generation.title: Restricted poly-A tailing (TdT) + RNase H
library_generation.summary: Terminal transferase adds a short poly(A) tail to the cDNA 3' end while RNase H removes the mRNA.
library_generation.note: 37C 50 sec (restricted time to keep the tail short) then 65C. Temperature is tightly controlled to avoid byproducts.

library_generation.title: Second-strand synthesis (tagging primer)
library_generation.summary: The tagging primer anneals to the new poly(A) tail and MightyAmp builds the second strand, placing the WTA tag at both ends.
library_generation.note: 98C->40C->68C. Both ends now carry the complementary M tag region, creating the substrate for suppression PCR.
library_generation.product:
double-stranded cDNA, tag at both ends:
5'- TATAGAATTCGCGGCCGCTCGCGAT...(T)24[cDNA] (A)n...ATCGCGAGCGGCCGCGAATTCTATA -3'
3'- ATATCTTAAGCGCCGGCGAGCGCTA...(A)24[cDNA'](T)n...TAGCGCTCGCCGGCGCTTAAGATAT -5'

library_generation.title: PCR enrichment (suppression PCR)
library_generation.summary: The single aminated suppression primer amplifies full-length cDNA; short byproducts self-anneal into a pan-like structure and are suppressed.
library_generation.note: MightyAmp, 16-21 cycles (98C/65C/68C). Complementary tag ends on short molecules pair intramolecularly and block priming.
library_generation.product:
amplified double-stranded cDNA (aminated tag at both ends):
5'- (NH2)GTATAGAATTCGCGGCCGCTCGCGAT......[cDNA] ......ATCGCGAGCGGCCGCGAATTCTATAC(NH2) -3'
3'- (NH2)CATATCTTAAGCGCCGGCGAGCGCTA......[cDNA']......TAGCGCTCGCCGGCGCTTAAGATATG(NH2) -5'

library_generation.title: Purification of amplified cDNA
library_generation.summary: Amplified cDNA is column-purified (MinElute / Zymo DNA Clean & Concentrator-5).
library_generation.note: ~63 ng cDNA obtained from 10 pg total RNA after 21 cycles; no byproducts, so no gel purification is needed.
library_generation.product: purified amplified WTA cDNA (~400-4000 bp), same structure as step 5

library_generation.title: Covaris fragmentation (LIMprep)
library_generation.summary: 20 ng amplified cDNA is sheared by focused ultrasonication to ~200-400 bp fragments.
library_generation.note: S220: duty 10%, PIP 175 W, 100 cycles/burst, 600 s. Terminal fragments still bear the 5'-aminated WTA tag, which blocks adapter ligation, so only internal cDNA fragments become sequenceable.

library_generation.title: End repair
library_generation.summary: KAPA End Repair blunts and phosphorylates fragment ends.
library_generation.note: 20C 30 min. Purified on DNA Clean & Concentrator-5 (Ampure XP avoided to retain <100 bp fragments).

library_generation.title: dA-tailing
library_generation.summary: KAPA A-tailing adds a single 3' A overhang to each end.

library_generation.title: TruSeq adapter ligation
library_generation.summary: Self-produced TruSeq Y-adapters (TRSU + indexed TRSI) are ligated to both ends; adapter dimers are removed with AMPure XP.
library_generation.note: 20C 15 min. The forked adapter puts the P5/Read1 arm on one end and the Read2/index/P7 arm on the other.

library_generation.title: Library PCR enrichment (TPC1 + TPC2)
library_generation.summary: KAPA HiFi with TPC1 (P5) and TPC2 (P7) amplifies adapter-ligated fragments into the final indexed paired-end library.
library_generation.note: 98C/60C/72C, typically 10-12 cycles; cycle number pre-determined by qPCR. AMPure XP cleanup; QC by BioAnalyzer + KAPA qPCR.

library_sequencing.note: Primer is identical to the P5-proximal top-strand region; it anneals to the bottom strand and reads one end of the cDNA insert 5'->3' into the fragment. Paired-end 50x2 on HiSeq 1000/2000.
Primer is identical to the top-strand Read-2 adaptor region; it anneals to the bottom strand and reads the 6-bp i7 index (e.g. CGATGT for TRSI-2).

library_sequencing.primer: TruSeq Read 2 sequencing primer (5'-GTGACTGGAGTTCAGACGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCT-3'; shown 3'->5' below its binding site)
library_sequencing.note: Primer anneals to the top strand at the P7-proximal Read-2 adaptor and extends leftward into the cDNA insert, reading the opposite end. Primer 3' end pairs at the insert/adaptor junction.

title: Quartz-Seq
description: Quartz-Seq is a plate-based, full-length single-cell RNA-seq / whole-transcript amplification (WTA) method. A single cell is lysed in a PCR tube and reverse-transcribed with an oligo-dT primer carrying a common tag (an EcoRI/NotI 'M' PCR-target region plus a T7 promoter); the RT primer is then digested with exonuclease I, the first-strand cDNA is given a restricted poly-A tail by terminal transferase, a tagging primer builds the second strand so both ends share the tag, and suppression PCR enriches full-length cDNA while eliminating short byproducts — all in one tube without purification. The amplified cDNA is Covaris-fragmented and converted into a paired-end TruSeq Illumina library (LIMprep). Reads capture cDNA fragments only (there are no cell barcodes or UMIs); sample multiplexing is provided by the TruSeq i7 index in the ligated adapter.

reference.label: Quartz-Seq: a highly reproducible and sensitive single-cell RNA sequencing method, reveals non-genetic gene-expression heterogeneity

original_publication.title: Quartz-Seq: a highly reproducible and sensitive single-cell RNA sequencing method, reveals non-genetic gene-expression heterogeneity

throughput.summary: Low-throughput, plate/tube-based single-cell WTA: one cell per PCR tube, one tube and 6 reaction steps per cell, all in a single tube without purification.
throughput.cells: Single cells FACS-sorted into 0.4 ul lysis buffer (or 10 pg diluted total RNA); ~6.8 pg total RNA/ES cell in G1, ~10 pg average across cell cycle; 98% amplification success (n=49).
throughput.rna: Validated on 10 pg total RNA and real single cells; reproducibly detects ~8,110 of ~9,873 transcripts (82.1%) at 10 pg.
throughput.dna: ~63 ng amplified cDNA from 10 pg total RNA after 21 PCR cycles.

publication.statistical_model: Pearson correlation and F-test for reproducibility; James-Stein empirical-Bayes mutual information (Kullback-Leibler) for differential expression. No UMI count model.

other.label: Amplification chemistry
other.value: Poly-A-tailing WTA (TdT) with suppression PCR, single tube, no purification, 6 reaction steps

other.label: Byproduct suppression
other.value: Exonuclease I digestion + restricted TdT time + aminated 'B' suppression PCR primer completely eliminate <200 bp byproducts (no gel purification)

other.label: Polymerase
other.value: MightyAmp DNA Polymerase (a.k.a. Terra PCR Direct); robust to GC-rich and single-tube crude lysate

other.label: Dual platform
other.value: T7 promoter in RT primer enables IVT-labelled cRNA for microarray (Quartz-Chip) in addition to Illumina sequencing (Quartz-Seq)

other.label: Reproducibility
other.value: Technical-replicate PCC ~0.93 at 10 pg total RNA (vs ~0.7 for Smart-Seq / CEL-Seq)

other.label: Library prep
other.value: LIMprep: Covaris fragmentation + self-produced TruSeq adapters (TRSU/TRSI) + KAPA HiFi PCR (TPC1/TPC2)

data_processing.summary: Raw reads are trimmed of the sequencing and WTA-adaptor primers, aligned to the mouse genome (mm9), and quantified as FPKM per transcript against an Ensembl transcript reference; samples are then hierarchically clustered and cell-state differences are called by mutual information. There are no cell barcodes or UMIs, so there is no demultiplex or UMI-collapse step and no molecular count model.
data_processing.statistical_model:
No UMI/molecular count model — FPKM abundance per transcript. Cell-state differential expression via Wilcoxon rank test plus a James-Stein empirical-Bayes estimator of mutual information (Kullback-Leibler); reproducible expression-fluctuation calling by F-test with FDR across replicate datasets; Pearson correlation for technical reproducibility.</statistical_model>
